questions: Are the 3 Run 4 CLI fixes (help text, comment IDs, error handling) correctly implemented?
Do the CLI commands follow existing codebase patterns?
Are there any correctness issues in the library module?

answers.answer: Yes — all 3 fixes verified. Help text includes 4 library commands between comments and skill sections. Comment IDs shown in parentheses for both top-level and reply output. Try-catch wrappers with user-friendly error messages on both comments-list and comments-post.
evidence: src/index.ts lines 54-57: library commands in usage()
src/library/comments-post.ts lines 62-76: try-catch with formatted error

answers.answer: Yes — the library module follows the same patterns as existing CLI modules. Router dispatching matches src/comments/index.ts. Flag parsing uses getFlag/requireFlag/getPositionalArgs from src/lib/flags.ts. HTTP calls use hxFetch from src/lib/http.ts with basePath: '/api'.
evidence: src/library/index.ts: same router pattern as src/comments/index.ts

answers.answer: No correctness issues found. The 3-format item resolution (cuid, short ID, title substring) works correctly with proper error messages for ambiguous or not-found cases. Rating aliases (up, down, love, thumbs-up, thumbs-down) map correctly. Auto-slugify handles heading text with spaces.
evidence: src/lib/resolve-library-item.ts lines 50-80: 3-strategy resolution with clear error messages
src/library/comments-post.ts lines 6-11: rating map with all aliases
src/library/comments-list.ts lines 17-24: slugify function matches rehype-slug output

statement: CLI implementation is clean and complete. No code changes needed from code review. All 3 Run 4 discoverability fixes are correctly implemented. The module follows established CLI patterns and handles errors gracefully. TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors.
